name: Michaelis-Menten Enzyme Kinetics
description: Michaelis-Menten kinetics models an enzyme binding substrate into an ES complex that turns over to product; under the steady-state assumption that [ES] is constant, the rate becomes v = Vmax[S]/(Km+[S]), and varying substrate concentration lets Km and Vmax be fitted from the saturation curve.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Enzyme + substrate"]
    N2["Form ES (E+S <=> ES)"]
    N3["ES complex"]
    N4{"Steady state assumed?"}
    N5["Pre-steady-state regime"]
    N6["d[ES]/dt ~ 0"]
    N7["Catalysis ES -> E + P"]
    N8["v = Vmax[S]/(Km+[S])"]
    N9["Vary [S], fit Km/Vmax"]
    N10["Kinetic parameters"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 -->|no| N5
    N5 -->|relax| N2
    N4 -->|yes| N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 --> N10

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: The original Michaelis constant: translation of the 1913 Michaelis-Menten paper
sources.authors: Johnson, K. A.; Goody, R. S.

sources.title: A note on the kinetics of enzyme action
sources.authors: Briggs, G. E.; Haldane, J. B. S.
